content: PWA install pitfalls: (1) iOS Safari does not implement beforeinstallprompt — no browser-native install banner; only way to install is manual Add to Home Screen from Safari share sheet. In-app browsers (Twitter webview, etc.) don't show the option at all. (2) Showing install instructions inside an in-app browser is useless — detect with navigator.standalone and user agent. (3) iOS storage: PWA installed to home screen is EXEMPT from ITP 7-day eviction rule (confirmed by WebKit); browser-only visits ARE subject to eviction. (4) iOS storage limit approx 50MB for Cache Storage in Safari; IndexedDB quota is separate. (5) iOS has no push notifications without permission (Safari 17+ on iOS 17+ added web push but requires explicit permission prompt). (6) navigator.storage.persist() available since Safari 17; requesting it on first run after install protects from OS-level eviction — grant likelihood higher if app is installed PWA.
